content:
Member-only story

From file handling and clipboard access to animations and observers, these native APIs made several of my favorite libraries unnecessary.

5 min read
Just now

--

Our bundle analyzer flagged something I didn’t want to see: forty percent of our vendor chunk was libraries doing things the browser had quietly learned to do on its own.
What we found sitting in there:

• A clipboard library

• A resize-detection polyfill

• An intersection-observer shim for a spec that shipped years ago
Press enter or click to view image in full size

We’d been importing solutions to problems the platform had already solved. Eleven packages and one audit later, here’s what actually replaced what — and where the native version still falls short.
Numbers below are from our own build: Webpack 5, production mode, gzip’d, measured with source-map-explorer on a mid-tier Android profile in Chrome DevTools' CPU throttling at 4x slowdown. Your mileage depends entirely on which of these libraries you're already carrying.
